hooks.command: input=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); if echo "$input" | grep -qiE "(AWS_|OPENAI_API_KEY|SECRET_KEY|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY)=[A-Za-z0-9]"; then echo '[HOOK BLOCKED] Potential secret detected in command. Use env vars instead.' >&2; exit 2; fi

path=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); if echo "$path" | grep -qE '\.env$'; then echo '[HOOK BLOCKED] Never write secrets to .env directly. Edit .env.example and let the user fill values.' >&2; exit 2; fi

path=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); if echo "$path" | grep -qE '\.(js|ts)$' && echo "$path" | grep -vqE '(test|spec|__tests__)'; then echo '[HOOK] New source file written. Consider: does this need a test file?'; fi
echo '[HOOK] Session complete. Reminders: (1) Run tests before committing. (2) Never commit .env. (3) Follow Conventional Commits. (4) Update docs/architecture.md if a design decision changed.'
